overview.title: Database Migration Best Practices
overview.description: Comprehensive best practices, common pitfalls, and troubleshooting guide for database migrations across platforms.

practice.title: Always Backup Before Migration
practice.description: Create complete backups of source database before starting migration
practice.rationale: Enables rollback if migration fails or produces unexpected results

practice.title: Test in Non-Production First
practice.description: Always test migration in development/staging before production
practice.rationale: Identifies issues before they impact production systems

practice.title: Validate Connectivity and Permissions
practice.description: Verify database connections and user permissions before migration

practice.title: Plan for Capacity and Performance
practice.description: Ensure target database has sufficient resources

considerations.item: Temporary space for sorting and indexing

practice.title: Use Phased Migration Approach
practice.description: Break migration into manageable phases with validation checkpoints
practice.rationale: Easier to identify and fix issues at each stage

practice.title: Process Data in Batches
practice.description: Migrate data in manageable batches rather than all at once

practice.title: Leverage Parallel Execution
practice.description: Use parallel threads for independent tables

practice.title: Optimize Index Creation Timing
practice.description: Create indexes after data load for better performance

practice.title: Monitor Progress and Performance
practice.description: Continuously monitor migration progress and system health

practice.title: Maintain Comprehensive Logs
practice.description: Log all operations with timestamps and details
practice.log_retention: Keep logs for at least 30 days post-migration

practice.title: Comprehensive Validation
practice.description: Thoroughly validate migrated data before cutover

practice.title: Performance Testing and Optimization
practice.description: Test and optimize query performance on target database

practice.title: Gather Database Statistics
practice.description: Update database statistics for optimal query planning

practice.title: Document Everything
practice.description: Create comprehensive documentation of migration process

pitfall.title: Data Type Mismatches
pitfall.description: Incorrect data type mappings causing data loss or errors

examples.example: Oracle NUMBER without precision → PostgreSQL INTEGER (may truncate large values)
SQL Server DATETIME → PostgreSQL DATE (loses time component)
MySQL TINYINT(1) → PostgreSQL SMALLINT (should be BOOLEAN)

pitfall.solution: Carefully review and test data type mappings before migration

pitfall.title: Character Encoding Issues
pitfall.description: Incorrect character encoding causing data corruption
pitfall.solution: Ensure consistent UTF-8 encoding throughout migration process

pitfall.title: NULL Value Handling

issues.issue: Oracle treats empty strings as NULL
SQL Server ISNULL vs PostgreSQL COALESCE

pitfall.solution: Explicitly handle NULL values in conversion logic

pitfall.title: Case Sensitivity Differences
pitfall.description: Different case sensitivity rules between databases
pitfall.solution: Use consistent quoting and case conventions

pitfall.title: Transaction Size Too Large
pitfall.description: Attempting to migrate too much data in single transaction
pitfall.solution: Use batch processing with appropriate transaction boundaries

pitfall.title: Foreign Key Constraint Violations
pitfall.description: Migrating child tables before parent tables, OR re-running migration when FK constraints from a prior run are still present (circular FK schemas).

solution.step: ALWAYS drop all FK constraints immediately before Phase 5 (data load)
Re-apply FK constraints in Phase 6 (post-data DDL) via pg_constraint NOT EXISTS guards
This is mandatory for re-runnable migrations — not optional

pitfall.example: Oracle HR schema: DEPARTMENTS.MANAGER_ID → EMPLOYEES, EMPLOYEES.DEPARTMENT_ID → DEPARTMENTS. Circular FK means neither table can load first if FKs are present. Solution: drop FKs before load, re-apply after. Phase 6 handles this unconditionally.

pitfall.title: psycopg2 copy_from() Double-Quoting Identifiers
pitfall.description: copy_from(table, columns) applies its own quoting internally. Passing pre-quoted identifiers results in double-quoting: "hr_schema"."TABLE" becomes ""hr_schema"."TABLE"". Tables created with double-quoted uppercase names cannot be found.

solution.step: NEVER use copy_from() for migrations with quoted identifiers
ALWAYS use copy_expert() with an explicit COPY SQL string you write
In copy_expert() you control all quoting — same rule as cur.execute()

pitfall.correct_pattern: col_expr = ", ".join(f'"{{c}}"' for c in columns) sql = f'COPY "{{schema}}"."{{table}}" ({{col_expr}}) FROM STDIN WITH (FORMAT text, DELIMITER E\'\\t\', NULL \'\\N\')' cur.copy_expert(sql, buf)

pitfall.title: Duplicate Key Violations on Re-run
pitfall.description: Not truncating target tables before data load causes duplicate PK violations when the migration is re-run. Gating truncate behind an env-var flag is wrong — it makes idempotency opt-in instead of guaranteed.

solution.step: ALWAYS truncate each target table unconditionally before loading
CASCADE is safe — FK constraints are dropped before load (Pre-Phase 5) and re-applied after (Phase 6), so no child rows exist at truncation time

pitfall.title: CHECK Constraint Column Name Case Mismatch
pitfall.description: Oracle stores search_condition with lowercase identifiers (e.g. "end_date > start_date") but PostgreSQL columns were created with double-quoted uppercase names ("END_DATE"). Quoting without uppercasing produces "end_date" which PostgreSQL cannot resolve.

solution.step: Token-level rewriter MUST uppercase each bare identifier before quoting it

pitfall.title: Bash 4+ Operators Fail on macOS (bash 3.2)
pitfall.description: macOS ships bash 3.2 (frozen since 2007 due to GPLv3). The parameter expansion operators ^^ (uppercase) and ,, (lowercase) are bash 4.0+ only and raise "bad substitution" on macOS, aborting the migration script.

solution.step: NEVER use ${VAR^^} or ${VAR,,} in any shell script
ALWAYS use tr for case conversion — it is POSIX-portable

pitfall.title: Creating Indexes Before Data Load
pitfall.description: Indexes slow down data insertion significantly
pitfall.solution: Create indexes after data load completes

pitfall.title: Insufficient Validation
pitfall.description: Not validating data thoroughly after migration

solutions.solution: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff
Check network configuration and firewall rules
Stream large objects instead of loading into memory

symptoms.symptom: Migration taking much longer than expected

solutions.solution: Drop indexes before load, recreate after
Use bulk load utilities (COPY, BULK INSERT)

symptoms.symptom: Data values differ between source and target

solutions.solution: Clean up data quality issues in source

method.name: COPY command
method.description: Fastest way to load data into PostgreSQL
method.example: COPY table_name FROM '/path/to/file.csv' WITH (FORMAT csv, HEADER true);

method.name: BULK INSERT
method.example: BULK INSERT table_name FROM '/path/to/file.csv' WITH (FIELDTERMINATOR = ',', ROWTERMINATOR = '\n');

optimization.title: Minimize Transaction Logging
optimization.description: Reduce logging overhead during bulk operations

techniques.technique: Use UNLOGGED tables for temporary staging

optimization.warning: Ensure backups are taken before disabling logging

optimization.title: Enable Parallel DML
optimization.description: Use parallel execution for data manipulation

optimization.title: Optimize Memory Settings
optimization.description: Tune database memory parameters for migration workload

parameter.name: work_mem
parameter.recommendation: Increase for sorting and hashing operations

consideration.title: Secure Credential Management
consideration.description: Never store credentials in code or version control

best_practices.practice: Use secret management systems (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager)

consideration.title: Data Encryption
consideration.description: Encrypt data in transit and at rest

consideration.title: Audit Logging
consideration.description: Maintain audit trail of migration activities

rollback_strategy.principle: Always have a rollback plan before starting migration

method.title: Restore from Backup
method.description: Restore source database from pre-migration backup

steps.step: Stop application access to target database

method.title: Reverse Migration
method.description: Migrate data back from target to source
method.when_to_use: Partial migration or data quality issues

requirements.requirement: Keep source database intact during migration

testing.test: Test rollback procedures before production migration
